name: Sheet Metal Press Brake Bending — Bending Force, Springback Angle & Minimum Bend Radius
subtitle: Sheet Metal | Metal Forming | Fabrication — DIN 6935:1975 (cold bending of flat bars), ISO 7438:2016 (bend testing)

inScope: Sheet Metal Press Brake Bending — Bending Force, Springback Angle & Minimum Bend Radius
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

standards.name: Sheet Metal Press Brake Bending — Bending Force, Springback Angle & Minimum Bend Radius
standards.description: Sheet Metal Press Brake Bending — Bending Force, Springback Angle & Minimum Bend Radius — Sheet Metal, Metal Forming, Fabrication

formulas.reference: Air bending: F = 1.33·R_m·t²·L/V — DIN 6935 / SME Sheet Metal Handbook §14.2; factor 1.33 for 90° V-bending

formulas.expression: E_mpa = 210000; elongation_pct = Math.max(5, (1 - Fy_mpa/Rm_mpa) * 60); r_min_mm = material_thickness_mm * (50 / elongation_pct - 1)
formulas.reference: r_min = t(50/A−1) — SME §14.3; A=elongation% from Rm/Re ratio; ISO 7438 §7.3 bend test criterion

formulas.expression: r_neutral_mm = punch_radius_mm + material_thickness_mm / 2; springback_factor = 1 - Fy_mpa * r_neutral_mm / (E_mpa * material_thickness_mm); Theta_punch_deg = bend_angle_deg / springback_factor; delta_theta_deg = Theta_punch_deg - bend_angle_deg
formulas.reference: Springback: θ_punch/θ_final = 1/(1 − 3·R_e·r/(E·t)) — Hosford & Caddell §7; simplified elastic recovery

formulas.expression: k_factor = (punch_radius_mm < 2 * material_thickness_mm) ? 0.33 : 0.41; BA_mm = (punch_radius_mm + k_factor * material_thickness_mm) * bend_angle_deg * Math.PI / 180
formulas.reference: BA = (r_p + K·t)·θ_rad — SME / Machinery's Handbook 32nd Ed.; K-factor: 0.33 (tight), 0.41 (standard), 0.50 (loose)
Bottom bending: F_bottom ≈ 3 × F_air_bending — SME §14.4; coining eliminates springback but requires 5–10× air-bending force
15% safety factor on calculated bending force; select press brake rated ≥ F_required; 1 tonne-force = 9.81 kN

validation.message: Punch radius < minimum bend radius: cracking risk on outer surface — increase punch radius to r_min or pre-heat material; verify with trial bends
Die opening < 6t: excessive bending force and tool wear — minimum V = 6t for aluminium, 8t for mild steel; smaller die opening risks sheet damage and machine overload
Required bending force > 1000 tonnes: very heavy press brake needed — verify material and geometry; consider multi-pass incremental bending or rolling for thick plates

warnings.message: Springback > 10°: significant overbend required — for high-strength steel and AHSS, springback can reach 15–25°; consider bottom bending or laser tracking closed-loop angle control on CNC press brake
High-strength material with large r_min: difficult-to-form sheet — consider hot-forming, pre-annealing, or laser scoring at bend line for UHSS > 700 MPa
Long bend + high force: press brake deflection significant — ram crowning compensation mandatory; deflection without crowning causes trapezoidal bends on long parts
Die opening > 16t: excessive — large V-opening reduces bending accuracy and increases springback; use V = 8–10t for best accuracy-to-force balance on structural steel
Large punch radius with shallow angle: wipe bending geometry — verify punch stroke and die sizing prevent material slip; consider pre-positioning stops

verdictText.warn: PASS — High Utilization: Peer Review Required

legalNotice: ⚠ Engineering decision support only — verify before use. This is a technical simulation, not professional engineering advice.
